prompt: isometric pixel-art game tiles for a top-down RPG map. Each tile is a DIFFERENT large object standing FREE on a small flat patch of packed brown dirt — the object itself rises straight up and IS the sprite. Do NOT put it on a raised block, cube, column, box, crate or pedestal, and do NOT draw the ground as a tall raised cube: just a small low patch at the base, everything else transparent. Go for maximum variety — different shapes, species, ages and conditions, no two tiles alike. The set: 1) a tall stack of balanced boulders 2) a rocky dirt spire 3) a tall termite spire 4) a tall dry cypress 5) a tall bare dead tree 6) a tall totem of stacked bones 7) a weathered wooden windmill 8) a dead snag draped with dry vines 9) a dirt cliff face with dangling roots 10) a wind-carved dirt hoodoo. cozy hand-crafted top-down RPG pixel art, rich natural texture, cohesive harmonious palette, soft directional shading, high quality game asset, transparent background
